ddls:
CREATE TABLE "power_consumption_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data point */,
  "hour" INTEGER /* Hour of the day (0-23) */,
  "time_sec" INTEGER /* Time in seconds */,
  "p1_kw" REAL /* Active power (kW) at point 1 */,
  "q1_kvar" REAL /* Reactive power (kvar) at point 1 */,
  "p2_kw" REAL /* Active power (kW) at point 2 */,
  "q2_kvar" REAL /* Reactive power (kvar) at point 2 */,
  "p3_kw" REAL /* Active power (kW) at point 3 */,
  "q3_kvar" REAL /* Reactive power (kvar) at point 3 */,
  "meter_id" INTEGER /* ID of the smart meter */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the IoT device */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the data point */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the data point */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_power_consumption_data_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "smart_meters" (
  "meter_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each smart meter */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer the meter belongs to */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the meter was installed */,
  "meter_type" TEXT /* Type of smart meter (e.g., single-phase, three-phase) */,
  "meter_model" TEXT /* Model of the smart meter */,
  "meter_serial_number" TEXT /* Serial number of the smart meter */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("meter_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_smart_meters_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "iot_devices" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each IoT device */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer the device belongs to */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the device was installed */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of IoT device (e.g., current transformer, voltage transformer) */,
  "device_model" TEXT /* Model of the IoT device */,
  "device_serial_number" TEXT /* Serial number of the IoT device */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_iot_devices_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the customer */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the customer */,
  "customer_type" TEXT /* Type of customer (residential, commercial, industrial) */,
  "peak_demand_kw" REAL /* Peak demand of the customer in kW */,
  "contracted_kw" REAL /* Contracted power of the customer in kW */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "power_quality_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each power quality metric */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data point the metric belongs to */,
  "voltage" REAL /* Voltage at the time of measurement */,
  "current" REAL /* Current at the time of measurement */,
  "power_factor" REAL /* Power factor at the time of measurement */,
  "frequency" REAL /* Frequency at the time of measurement */,
  "harmonics" REAL /* Harmonics at the time of measurement */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_power_quality_metrics_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES power_consumption_data ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "power_outages" (
  "outage_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each power outage */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the outage */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the outage */,
  "affected_customers" INTEGER /* Number of customers affected by the outage */,
  "outage_type" TEXT /* Type of outage (e.g., scheduled, unscheduled) */,
  "outage_cause" TEXT /* Cause of the outage */,
  "outage_resolution" TEXT /* Resolution of the outage */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("outage_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer the notification was sent to */,
  "notification_time" TEXT /* Time the notification was sent */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message sent to the customer */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., outage, maintenance) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., customer service, engineer, admin) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login timestamp of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_sessions" (
  "session_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each session */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "session_start" TEXT /* Start time of the session */,
  "session_end" TEXT /* End time of the session */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("session_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_requests" (
  "request_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each request */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who made the request */,
  "request_time" TEXT /* Time the request was made */,
  "request_type" TEXT /* Type of request (e.g., meter reading, outage report) */,
  "request_status" TEXT /* Status of the request (e.g., pending, resolved) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("request_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "meter_readings" (
  "reading_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each reading */,
  "meter_id" INTEGER /* ID of the meter */,
  "reading_time" TEXT /* Time the reading was taken */,
  "reading_value" REAL /* Value of the reading */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reading_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "energy_usage" (
  "usage_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each usage record */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "usage_date" TEXT /* Date of the usage */,
  "usage_value" REAL /* Value of the usage */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("usage_id")
)
